CREATE TABLE test_strings (id INT, str_value VARCHAR(255));
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (2, 'This is a test string for substrings.');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (3, 'Example string containing the substring.');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (4, 'Another test for substring search.');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (5, 'Substring at the very start.');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (6, 'No match here.');
INSERT INTO test_strings (id, str_value) VALUES (7, 'abrA should be found case insensitively.');
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, 'abrA') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE strstr(str_value, 'abrA') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, 'substring') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE strstr(str_value, 'substring') >= 0;
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, 'found') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE id IN (SELECT id FROM test_strings WHERE strstr(str_value, 'found') >= 0);
SELECT ts1.id, ts1.str_value, strstr(ts2.str_value, 'test') AS found_index FROM test_strings ts1 JOIN test_strings ts2 ON ts1.id <> ts2.id WHERE strstr(ts2.str_value, 'test') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, '') AS found_index FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, strstr(NULL, 'test') AS found_index FROM test_strings;
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, 'test') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE strchr(str_value, ' ') IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, strstr(str_value, 'for') AS found_index FROM test_strings WHERE id = (SELECT MIN(id) FROM test_strings WHERE strstr(str_value, 'for') IS NOT NULL);
SELECT id, strstr(NULL, NULL) AS found_index FROM test_strings;
